title: Residential Split System Installation
description: A technician installed a residential split-system AC. Review the listed components and identify what's missing.
presentItems: Outdoor condensing unit on concrete pad
Indoor evaporator coil mounted on furnace
Copper refrigerant line set (suction and liquid)

missingItems.name: Condensate drain line with P-trap
missingItems.explanation: Every evaporator coil produces condensate that must be drained. A P-trap prevents conditioned air from being pulled through the drain line. Required by IMC 307.2.

missingItems.name: Refrigerant line insulation
missingItems.explanation: The suction line must be insulated to prevent condensation and maintain system efficiency. Required by IMC 1104.1.

explanation: A complete split system installation requires proper condensate drainage and refrigerant line insulation. Missing either causes water damage or reduced efficiency.

title: Gas Furnace Installation
description: A gas furnace has been installed in a utility closet. Check the installation for missing components.
presentItems: Gas supply line with shutoff valve

missingItems.name: Sediment trap (drip leg)
missingItems.explanation: A sediment trap (drip leg) must be installed as close to the equipment as practical. It catches debris and moisture before they enter the gas valve. Required by IFGC 408.4.

missingItems.name: Combustion air openings
missingItems.explanation: A furnace in a confined space requires combustion air openings — typically two openings, one within 12 inches of the top and one within 12 inches of the bottom. Required by IFGC 304.

explanation: Gas furnaces require a sediment trap to protect the gas valve and adequate combustion air openings in confined spaces. Both are code requirements that protect safety.

title: Bathroom Exhaust Fan
description: A bathroom exhaust fan was installed in a new construction home. Identify what's missing.
presentItems: Flexible duct from fan to roof cap

missingItems.name: Backdraft damper
missingItems.explanation: A backdraft damper prevents outside air, insects, and moisture from entering through the exhaust duct when the fan is off. Required by IMC 501.2.

missingItems.name: Makeup air pathway
missingItems.explanation: Exhaust fans remove air from the space, which must be replaced. In tight construction, a dedicated makeup air path or transfer grille is needed. Required by IRC M1507.3.

explanation: Bathroom exhaust installations need a backdraft damper to prevent reverse airflow and a makeup air pathway to replace exhausted air, especially in modern tight-construction homes.

title: Residential Duct System
description: A new residential duct system has been roughed in. Check for missing components.
presentItems: Sheet metal trunk line from furnace

missingItems.name: Duct support hangers at proper intervals
missingItems.explanation: Horizontal ducts must be supported at intervals not exceeding 10 feet. Without proper support, ducts sag and restrict airflow. Required by SMACNA standards and IMC 603.2.

missingItems.name: Return air filter rack or grille
missingItems.explanation: A filter rack or filter-back grille is required to protect the equipment from debris and maintain air quality. Required by IMC 606.2.

explanation: Residential duct systems require proper support to prevent sagging and a filter to protect equipment. Both are basic requirements that affect system performance and longevity.

title: Rooftop Unit Installation
description: A 10-ton rooftop packaged unit has been installed on a commercial building. Review the installation for missing items.
presentItems: Gas line with shutoff valve and drip leg
Condensate drain piped to roof drain

missingItems.name: Vibration isolation curb or pads
missingItems.explanation: Rooftop units generate significant vibration that transmits through the building structure. Vibration isolation prevents noise complaints and structural fatigue. Required by ASHRAE guidelines.

missingItems.name: Roof curb rain hood/flashing
missingItems.explanation: The junction between curb and roof membrane must be properly flashed and sealed to prevent water infiltration. Missing flashing leads to roof leaks. Required by SMACNA Architectural Sheet Metal Manual.

explanation: Rooftop installations require vibration isolation to prevent noise/structural issues and proper curb flashing to prevent roof leaks. Both protect the building long-term.

title: Commercial Air Handler Installation
description: A commercial AHU has been installed in a mechanical room. Identify the missing components.
presentItems: Air handling unit with blower, cooling coil, and heating coil
Chilled water piping to cooling coil
Hot water piping to heating coil
Control panel with VFD for fan motor

missingItems.name: Access doors for coil and filter service
missingItems.explanation: Access doors must be provided upstream and downstream of coils and at filter sections for maintenance. Without them, filters cannot be changed and coils cannot be cleaned. Required by SMACNA standards.

missingItems.name: Smoke detector in return air duct
missingItems.explanation: Duct smoke detectors are required in systems over 2,000 CFM to shut down the fan upon smoke detection and prevent smoke spread through the building. Required by NFPA 90A and IMC 606.4.

explanation: Commercial AHU installations require access doors for maintenance and duct smoke detectors for life safety. Both are code requirements for systems above 2,000 CFM.

title: Chilled Water Piping System
description: A chilled water piping loop has been installed for a commercial cooling system. Check for missing components.
presentItems: Chilled water supply and return piping

missingItems.name: Expansion tank
missingItems.explanation: As water temperature changes, it expands and contracts. An expansion tank absorbs this volume change and prevents dangerous pressure buildup. Required by IMC 1005.1.

missingItems.name: Air separator or air vent
missingItems.explanation: Dissolved air in chilled water causes corrosion, noise, and reduced heat transfer. An air separator removes entrained air from the system. Required by ASHRAE Handbook — HVAC Systems and Equipment.

explanation: Hydronic systems require expansion tanks to handle thermal expansion and air separators to remove entrained air. Both are essential for reliable, efficient operation.

title: Mini-Split Indoor Unit
description: A ductless mini-split indoor wall unit has been installed in a bedroom addition. Identify what's missing.

missingItems.name: Condensate pump or overflow safety switch
missingItems.explanation: When the indoor unit is not high enough above a drain point, a condensate pump is needed. At minimum, an overflow safety switch prevents water damage if the drain clogs. Required by IMC 307.2.2.

missingItems.name: Wall sleeve seal and weatherproofing
missingItems.explanation: The wall penetration for refrigerant lines must be sealed with a proper wall sleeve and caulk to prevent air infiltration, moisture intrusion, and pest entry. Required by manufacturer installation guides.

explanation: Mini-split installations require condensate overflow protection to prevent water damage and proper wall penetration sealing to maintain the building envelope.

title: Kitchen Hood Exhaust System
description: A Type I commercial kitchen hood has been installed over a cooking line. Check for missing components.
presentItems: Stainless steel Type I hood with grease filters

missingItems.name: Makeup air unit
missingItems.explanation: Kitchen exhaust removes large volumes of air that must be replaced. A dedicated makeup air unit prevents negative pressure, back-drafting of gas appliances, and door slamming. Required by IMC 508.

missingItems.name: Grease duct cleanout access
missingItems.explanation: Grease duct systems must have cleanout/access openings at direction changes and at 20-foot intervals for inspection and cleaning. Required by IMC 506.3.12.

explanation: Commercial kitchen hoods require makeup air to replace exhausted air and cleanout access in the grease duct for regular fire safety maintenance.

title: VAV Box Installation
description: A pressure-independent VAV terminal box has been installed above the ceiling. Identify missing components.
presentItems: VAV box with actuator and controller

missingItems.name: Flexible duct connection at inlet
missingItems.explanation: A flexible duct connector at the VAV box inlet isolates fan vibration and noise from transferring through the duct system to the occupied space. Required by SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards.

missingItems.name: Balancing damper downstream
missingItems.explanation: A manual balancing damper is needed downstream to fine-tune airflow to each diffuser branch. Without it, air distribution cannot be properly balanced. Required by ASHRAE Standard 111.

explanation: VAV box installations need flexible connections for vibration isolation and balancing dampers for proper air distribution to the served zones.

title: Clean Room HVAC System
description: An ISO Class 7 clean room HVAC system has been installed. Check for missing components in this critical application.
presentItems: Dedicated AHU with high-efficiency cooling coil
HEPA filter terminal units at ceiling
BAS controls with temperature and humidity sensors
Positive room pressure relative to adjacent spaces

missingItems.name: Pre-filter bank upstream of HEPA filters
missingItems.explanation: Pre-filters (MERV 8 minimum, typically MERV 13-14) must be installed upstream to extend the life of expensive HEPA filters. Without pre-filtration, HEPA filters load prematurely. Required by ISO 14644 guidelines.

missingItems.name: Room pressure differential monitor with alarm
missingItems.explanation: Clean rooms must maintain a minimum positive pressure differential (typically 0.03-0.05 in. W.C.). A pressure monitor with alarm alerts staff when containment is compromised. Required by ISO 14644-4.

missingItems.name: Airlock or anteroom at entry point
missingItems.explanation: An airlock or anteroom with interlocked doors prevents contamination during personnel entry. It serves as a buffer zone between the clean room and adjacent spaces. Required by ISO 14644-4.

explanation: Clean room HVAC systems have stricter requirements than standard systems. Pre-filtration protects HEPA filters, pressure monitoring ensures containment, and airlocks prevent contamination during entry.

title: Window AC Unit Installation
description: A window air conditioning unit has been installed in a bedroom. Check for missing items.
presentItems: Window AC unit seated in window frame
Power cord plugged into wall outlet
Unit leveled with slight rear tilt

missingItems.name: Side panel seals and weatherstripping
missingItems.explanation: The gaps between the unit and window frame must be sealed with the included side panels and weatherstripping to prevent air infiltration, rain entry, and insect intrusion.

missingItems.name: Support bracket or angle
missingItems.explanation: Units must be securely supported. Heavier units require an exterior support bracket to prevent the unit from falling. Recommended by manufacturer installation guidelines and building codes.

explanation: Window AC installations need proper side panel sealing to prevent air and water infiltration, and support brackets for safety. These simple steps are often skipped but are important.

title: Commercial Duct Run in Seismic Zone
description: A large commercial duct system has been installed in a seismically active area. Review the listed components and identify what's missing.
presentItems: 24x24 galvanized steel supply duct with turning vanes
Trapeze hangers at proper spacing per SMACNA
Fire dampers at all rated wall penetrations
Duct sealed with mastic at all joints

missingItems.name: Seismic bracing on duct run
missingItems.explanation: Ductwork in seismic zones must have lateral and longitudinal seismic bracing per ASCE 7, SMACNA Seismic Restraint Manual, and IBC requirements. Without seismic bracing, duct runs can swing, collapse, or separate at joints during an earthquake, blocking egress paths and compromising fire/smoke barriers.

missingItems.name: Fire damper at rated wall penetration
missingItems.explanation: All duct penetrations through fire-rated wall and floor assemblies require listed fire dampers matching the assembly rating. Missing fire dampers allow fire and smoke to spread between compartments, defeating the building's fire protection strategy. Required by IMC 607.3.

explanation: Seismic bracing and fire dampers are critical life-safety components in commercial duct installations. UCSB specs mandate seismic restraint per ASCE 7 and fire dampers at every rated penetration. Both are frequently missed during installation and caught during final inspection.

title: Kitchen Exhaust System Components
description: A Type I commercial kitchen exhaust system has been installed. Check for missing components required by the UCSB specification.
presentItems: Type I grease hood over cooking appliances
Upblast exhaust fan with grease drain
Automatic fire suppression system in hood
Makeup air unit providing tempered supply

missingItems.name: Fire barrier wrap on grease duct (minimum 1.5 inches per ASTM E2336)
missingItems.explanation: Grease ducts passing through building spaces must be wrapped with fire barrier material tested to ASTM E2336 / UL 2221 at a minimum thickness of 1.5 inches. This protects the building structure from a grease duct fire, which can reach temperatures exceeding 2000°F.

missingItems.name: Cleanout access doors at every direction change
missingItems.explanation: Grease ducts require cleanout access openings at every change in direction and at horizontal sections to allow thorough cleaning. Grease buildup in inaccessible duct sections is a leading cause of commercial kitchen fires. Required by IMC 506.3.9.

explanation: Commercial kitchen exhaust systems are high-fire-risk installations. UCSB specs require fire barrier wrap meeting ASTM E2336 at 1.5-inch minimum thickness and cleanout access at every direction change. These protect the building and enable the regular cleaning required to prevent grease fires.
